| MCP:HMg9Sb | # SpringBoot Java Template | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | [![Twitter](https://img.shields.io/twitter/url?style=social&url=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Fgeminikims)](https://twitter.com/geminikims) | MCP:HMg9Sb | [![Youtube](https://img.shields.io/youtube/channel/views/UCDh8zEDofOcrOMAOnSVL9Tg?label=Youtube&style=social)](https://www.youtube.com/@geminikims) | MCP:HMg9Sb | [![CI](https://github.com/team-dodn/spring-boot-java-template/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/team-dodn/spring-boot-java-template/actions/workflows/ci.yml) | MCP:HMg9Sb | [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-green.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | This is not the best structure. This is a good basic structure to use early in the project when productivity is important. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | Remember, as your software grows, your structure must grow too. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | # **Modules** | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## Core | MCP:HMg9Sb | Each submodule of this module is responsible for one domain service. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | This must make the modular structure grow with the growth of the service. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ### core:core-api | MCP:HMg9Sb | It is the only executable module in the project. It is structured to have domains to maximize initial development productivity. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | It is also responsible for providing APIs and setting up frameworks for services. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ### core:core-enum | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | This module contains enums that are used by `core-api` and must be delivered to external modules. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb |
| MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## Clients | MCP:HMg9Sb | Submodules of this module are responsible for integrating with external systems. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ### clients:clients-example | MCP:HMg9Sb | This module shows an example of HTTP communication with `Spring-Cloud-Open-Feign`. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb |
| MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## Storage | MCP:HMg9Sb | Submodules of this module are responsible for integrating with the various storages. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ### storage:db-core | MCP:HMg9Sb | This module shows an example of connecting to `MySql` using `Spring-Data-JPA`. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb |
| MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## Support | MCP:HMg9Sb | Submodules of this module are responsible for additional support. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ### support:logging | MCP:HMg9Sb | This module supports logging of service and has a dependency added for distributed tracing support. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | It also includes dependencies to support `Sentry`. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ### support:monitoring | MCP:HMg9Sb | This module supports monitoring of services. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb |
| MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## Tests | MCP:HMg9Sb | Submodules of this module are responsible for the convenience of writing test codes. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ### tests:api-docs | MCP:HMg9Sb | This module is for writing spring-rest-docs conveniently. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb |
| MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | # Dependency Management | MCP:HMg9Sb | All dependency versioning is done through `gradle.properties` file. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | If you want to add a new dependency, put the version in `gradle.properties` and load it in `build.gradle`. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb |
| MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | # Runtime Profiles | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## local | MCP:HMg9Sb | This profile aims to configure an environment that can be developed even if the network is disconnected. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## local-dev | MCP:HMg9Sb | This profile aims configurations that allow me to connect to the DEV environment from my local machine. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## dev | MCP:HMg9Sb | This profile exists for deploying Development environments. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## staging | MCP:HMg9Sb | This profile exists for deploying Staging environments. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## live | MCP:HMg9Sb | This profile exists for deploying Live environments. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb |
| MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | # Test Tasks & Tags | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## test | MCP:HMg9Sb | This is a collection of test-tasks that we want to run on `CI`. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | If you want to change the settings, modify the `build.gradle` file. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## unitTest | MCP:HMg9Sb | This is a group of tests that typically have no dependencies, are fast to run, and test a single feature. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## contextTest | MCP:HMg9Sb | This is a task that runs with SpringContext and has integration tests. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## restDocsTest | MCP:HMg9Sb | This is a task to create asciidoc based on spring-rest-docs. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## developTest | MCP:HMg9Sb | This is a task of tests that should not be run in `CI`. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | This is a good tag to use if you're not good at writing tests. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb |
| MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | # Recommended Preferences | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## Git Hook | MCP:HMg9Sb | This setting makes run `lint` on every commit. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ``` | MCP:HMg9Sb | $ git config core.hookspath .githooks | MCP:HMg9Sb | ``` | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ## IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:HMg9Sb | This setting makes it easier to run the `test code` out of the box. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | ``` | MCP:HMg9Sb | // Gradle Build and run with IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:HMg9Sb | Build, Execution, Deployment > Build Tools > Gradle > Run tests using > IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:HMg9Sb | ``` | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | If you want to apply lint settings to the format of IDEA, please refer to the guide below. | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | [Spring Java Format IntelliJ IDEA](https://github.com/spring-io/spring-javaformat#intellij-idea) | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | --- | MCP:HMg9Sb | | MCP:HMg9Sb | # Supported By | MCP:HMg9Sb |
| MCP:HMg9Sb | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: ae2fee1a95d4ef3b1bae148cbdbc4f0d9726b694)

| MCP:HMg9Sb | $GIT_DIR/gradlew checkFormat

| MCP:HMg9Sb | applicationVersion=0.0.1-SNAPSHOT

| MCP:HMg9Sb |

| MCP:HMg9Sb | springCloudDependenciesVersion=2025.1.0

data.content:
Here's a summary based on the repository's **README.md**, **build.gradle**, **lint.gradle**, **.githooks/pre-commit**, and **gradle.properties**.

---

## Runtime Profiles

The template defines **five** Spring Boot profiles, each tailored to a deployment phase:

| Profile | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **`local`** | Fully offline-capable development environment—no network dependencies required. |
| **`local-dev`** | Local machine connects to the shared **DEV** environment (e.g., shared databases or services). |
| **`dev`** | Used when deploying to the **Development** environment. |
| **`staging`** | Used when deploying to the **Staging** environment. |
| **`live`** | Used when deploying to the **Production / Live** environment. |

These profiles let developers seamlessly switch between isolated local work, shared dev resources, and progressive deployment targets.

---

## Recommended Developer Setup (Linting & Testing Locally)

### 1. Linting — Automatic on Every Commit

The project uses **Spring Java Format** (`io.spring.javaformat`, version `0.0.47`) for code style enforcement.

- A **pre-commit Git hook** (`.githooks/pre-commit`) runs `./gradlew checkFormat` before every commit to verify formatting.
- To activate it, run once in your local clone:

  ```bash
  git config core.hookspath .githooks
  ```

- If you use **IntelliJ IDEA** and want the IDE to auto-format according to the same rules, install the [Spring Java Format IntelliJ IDEA plugin](https://github.com/spring-io/spring-javaformat#intellij-idea).

### 2. Running Tests

The Gradle build defines four test suites via JUnit 5 tags:

| Task / Tag | What It Covers |
|---|---|
| `unitTest` | Fast, isolated unit tests (excludes `develop`, `context`, and `restdocs` tags). |
| `contextTest` | Integration tests that spin up a Spring context (includes only the `context` tag). |
| `restDocsTest` | Spring REST Docs tests that generate AsciiDoc API documentation (includes only the `restdocs` tag). |
| `developTest` | Work-in-progress tests excluded from CI (includes only the `develop` tag). |

The default `test` task (run on CI) excludes `develop` and `restdocs` tags.

**To run tests locally:**

```bash
# All CI tests
./gradlew test

# Unit tests only
./gradlew unitTest

# Context / integration tests only
./gradlew contextTest

# REST Docs tests (also generates AsciiDoc)
./gradlew restDocsTest

# Developer-only scratch tests
./gradlew developTest
```

**IntelliJ IDEA tip:** Set **Build, Execution, Deployment → Build Tools → Gradle → Run tests using → IntelliJ IDEA** so that test run/debug configurations work out of the box from the IDE.

### 3. Key Build Details

- **Java version**: 25 (configured via `gradle.properties` and enforced through the Gradle toolchain).
- **Dependency versions**: Centralised in `gradle.properties` (`springBootVersion=4.0.0`, `springCloudDependenciesVersion=2025.1.0`, etc.).
